issues.description: General Notes numbering skips Note 16 (jumps from 15 to 17).
Sheet index lists multiple unrelated/contradictory TAs (e.g., Full Road Closure, Median Crossover) for a project scoped as a 'Single Lane Closure'.
issues.fix: Remove unused sheets from the plan set and update the index, or clearly label them as 'Optional/Contingency TAs'.

issues.description: Engineering Judgment Failure: Applying TA-10 (Flaggers) to a 3-lane road with a TWLTL is inappropriate. The closed bottom lane should be shifted into the TWLTL to maintain two-way traffic without flaggers. Using flaggers unnecessarily cuts capacity to 1,400 vph, failing the peak demand of 1,539 vph.
issues.fix: Redesign the traffic control strategy using a lane shift into the TWLTL (e.g., TA-31) instead of flaggers.

issues.description: The opposing flagger is shown standing in an open travel lane, stopping traffic unnecessarily since the work area only occupies the bottom lane.
issues.fix: Remove flaggers. If flaggers must be used, ensure they are controlling a truly shared single lane.

issues.description: W3-5 sign is only shown on the primary approach, but the sign schedule indicates it should be on both approaches.
issues.fix: Ensure the drawing matches the sign schedule.

positives: Sign spacing and taper lengths correctly reflect urban low-speed requirements.

issues.description: Title is 'FULL ROAD CLOSURE', but the diagram only shows the bottom lane and TWLTL closed, leaving the top lane open. A full closure must close all lanes.
issues.fix: Correct the diagram to span channelizing devices across the entire roadway.

issues.description: Sheet depicts a 'Median Crossover' (TA-18), but the roadway is defined as having a center turn lane (TWLTL), not a physical median.
issues.fix: Remove this sheet as it is geometrically incompatible with the project site.

issues.description: Sheet is titled 'DOUBLE LANE CLOSURE' but uses the exact same drawing as the Full Road Closure (Sheet 3), complete with 'ROAD CLOSED' and 'DETOUR' signs. A double lane closure on a 3-lane road leaves 1 lane open and does not require a detour.
issues.fix: Redraw the typical application to correctly show a double lane closure with a flagging operation for the remaining open lane.

positives: Clear coordinate mapping and intersection identification.
Properly scaled with a North arrow.

issues.description: The intersection details (Sheets 10-15) show the cross-street warning signs but completely omit the mainline work area and channelizing devices. Contractors need to know how cones are routed through the intersections.
issues.fix: Overlay the mainline lane closure footprint and cone placement onto the intersection diagrams.

positives: Notes explicitly address sight distance and cross-street sign requirements.

issues.description: The analysis correctly identifies that a flagging operation will cause a V/C ratio of 1.10 and a 3,558 ft queue. However, the plan still permits daytime work windows. A PE cannot stamp a plan that knowingly fails capacity without mandating night-work ONLY.
issues.fix: Change the TCP design to a TWLTL lane shift to increase capacity. If flagging is absolutely required, change the allowable work windows to 'Nighttime Only'.

positives: Calculations for demand vs. capacity are mathematically sound.
Crash history notes are highly relevant.
Excellent notes on emergency access, pedestrian accommodations, and nighttime operations.

issues.description: Sign quantities contradict the diagrams. W3-5 is listed as Qty 3 overall (1 for primary, 2 for 'Both approaches'), which is contradictory and doesn't match Sheet 2.
issues.fix: Reconcile the sign schedule quantities to exactly match the finalized typical application drawings.

positives: Sign sizes (36x36) are correct for the speed limit.

formatting_issues.description: Text inside the sign diamonds on the typical application drawings is overlapping and cramped.
formatting_issues.fix: Use standard MUTCD sign symbol graphics instead of writing full text strings inside the diamond shapes on the schematics.

missing_for_stamp: Complete redesign of the primary TCP strategy: Do not use flaggers (TA-10) for a single lane closure on a TWLTL roadway. Shift traffic into the center turn lane to maintain two-way traffic flow and prevent the massive 3,500 ft queue.
Removal or correction of erroneously titled sheets (Sheets 3, 4, 6) which contain copy-pasted or geometrically impossible drawings.
Update intersection details to show the interaction between the mainline closure cones and cross-street traffic.
Correction of the Sign Schedule to match the actual drawings.
Correction of the numbering typo in the General Notes.
conditions_for_stamp: The traffic control strategy must be changed to utilize the center turn lane.
The V/C ratio must be brought below 1.0 during allowable work windows.
All duplicate/incorrect standard drawings must be removed.
